fact: Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
Bananas are technically berries, while strawberries are not.
A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about 6 billion tons.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time — a phenomenon called the triple point.
Lightning strikes the Earth about 8 million times a day.
The human body contains enough carbon to make roughly 9,000 pencils.
Glass is not a solid or a liquid — it is an amorphous solid with properties of both.
Sound travels about 4 times faster in water than in air.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast about 100,000 slices of bread.
Diamonds can be made from peanut butter under extreme pressure.
Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
Hot water freezes faster than cold water — a phenomenon called the Mpemba effect.
Your stomach gets a new lining every three to four days to prevent it from digesting itself.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The average human body carries about 2 kilograms of bacteria.
Sunlight takes approximately 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
The Earth's core is about as hot as the surface of the Sun — around 5,500°C.
A photon of light takes about 40,000 years to travel from the Sun's core to its surface.
Human bones are approximately 5 times stronger than steel of the same density.
The total length of all blood vessels in the human body is about 100,000 kilometers.
An adult human body produces about 25 million new cells every second.
The Eiffel Tower can grow by up to 15 centimeters in summer due to thermal expansion of iron.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way — approximately 3 trillion trees.
Helium was first discovered on the Sun before it was found on Earth.
The human nose can detect over 1 trillion different scents.
If you could fold a piece of paper 42 times, it would reach the Moon.
A cloud can weigh more than 1 million pounds.
DNA can survive for over 500,000 years in the right conditions.
The temperature inside a lightning bolt is about 30,000 Kelvin — five times hotter than the Sun's surface.
Oxygen has a color — in its liquid and solid forms, it is pale blue.
The human brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy despite being only 2% of its weight.
A single human cell contains approximately 6 feet of DNA.
Saturn would float if placed in water because its average density is less than that of water.
The speed of light is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
The shortest war in history lasted just 38 to 45 minutes — between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896.
Stomach acid is strong enough to dissolve metal — it has a pH between 1 and 3.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the known universe.
Infrared light was the first invisible form of electromagnetic radiation to be discovered, in 1800.
The human eye can distinguish about 10 million different colors.
Gravity travels at the speed of light, according to Einstein's theory of general relativity.
A cockroach can live for up to a week without its head.
Your heart beats about 100,000 times a day, pumping about 7,500 liters of blood.
The Andromeda Galaxy is on a collision course with the Milky Way — they will merge in about 4.5 billion years.
Water is the only natural substance that exists in all three physical states — solid, liquid, and gas — at normal Earth temperatures.
It would take over 1 million Earths to fill up the Sun.
The average person walks about 160,000 kilometers in their lifetime — enough to walk around the Earth 4 times.
Every atom in your body is billions of years old — forged inside ancient stars.
Laser stands for Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation.
The largest known living organism is a honey fungus in Oregon that covers about 2,385 acres.
Your brain generates enough electrical power to light a small LED bulb — about 12 to 25 watts.
Metallic hydrogen, believed to exist inside Jupiter, could be a room-temperature superconductor.
The deepest point in the ocean, the Mariana Trench, is about 11 kilometers deep.
A single gram of DNA can store 700 terabytes of data.
The Earth rotates at about 1,670 kilometers per hour at the equator.
Antibiotics have no effect on viruses — they only work against bacteria.
About 99% of all species that have ever lived on Earth are now extinct.
The Sun loses about 4 million tons of mass every second due to nuclear fusion.
A human sneeze can travel at speeds of up to 160 kilometers per hour.
Mercury and Venus are the only two planets in our solar system that have no moons.
Humans glow in the dark, but the light is 1,000 times weaker than what the eye can detect.
The Sahara Desert was green and lush just 6,000 years ago.
A fetus develops fingerprints at three months — and they never change throughout life.
Neutron stars can spin at up to 716 times per second.
Ice is about 9% less dense than liquid water, which is why it floats.
One million seconds is about 11.5 days, while one billion seconds is about 31.7 years.
The chemical element Francium is so rare that there are only about 20 to 30 grams of it in the Earth's crust at any time.
Coral reefs support about 25% of all marine species despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor.
Radio waves from the first TV broadcasts have already traveled past 100,000 stars.
Venus spins in the opposite direction to most planets — the Sun rises in the west and sets in the east.
A hummingbird's heart beats up to 1,260 times per minute.
Black holes are not empty space — they are extremely dense objects with gravity so strong that light cannot escape.
The world's oceans contain about 20 million tons of dissolved gold.
Every year, the Moon moves about 3.8 centimeters farther from Earth.
Galileo was the first person to observe Saturn's rings through a telescope in 1610.
A tablespoon of a white dwarf star would weigh approximately 15 tons.
The Amazon Rainforest produces about 6% of the world's oxygen.
Graphene is the strongest material ever tested — 200 times stronger than steel.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The speed of sound increases with temperature — it travels faster in warm air.
Pluto has not yet completed a full orbit around the Sun since its discovery in 1930 — one orbit takes 248 years.
The pH scale was invented by Danish chemist Søren Sørensen in 1909.
There are more bacteria in your mouth than there are people on Earth.
Light from some stars visible today started its journey before the dinosaurs went extinct.
Absolute zero, the coldest possible temperature, is -273.15°C or 0 Kelvin.
The human skeleton is completely replaced every 10 years through a process called remodeling.
A rainbow is actually a full circle — we only see a semi-circle because the ground gets in the way.
The element mercury is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature and pressure.
Your body produces about 1 to 1.5 liters of saliva every day.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye — this is a common myth.
Earthquakes can make the Earth ring like a bell — scientists call these free oscillations.
The average cumulus cloud weighs about 500,000 kilograms.
Your corneas are the only part of your body that receive no blood supply — they get oxygen directly from the air.
The oldest known fossils are about 3.5 billion years old — cyanobacteria found in Western Australia.
Steel wool can be set on fire with a 9-volt battery.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum — space is completely silent.
The strongest muscle in the human body relative to its size is the masseter, or jaw muscle.
Approximately 71% of the Earth's surface is covered by water.
A single solar flare can release the energy equivalent of millions of nuclear bombs.
Penicillin, the first antibiotic, was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 from a mold called Penicillium notatum.
The hottest planet in the solar system is Venus, despite Mercury being closer to the Sun, due to its dense atmosphere trapping heat.
Sound travels approximately four times faster in water than it does in air.
A single strand of spider silk is stronger than a steel strand of the same thickness.
The Earth's core is estimated to be roughly the same temperature as the surface of the Sun.
There are more atoms in a single grain of salt than there are stars in the observable universe.
The blue whale's heart is about the size of a bumper car.
Light takes approximately eight minutes and twenty seconds to travel from the Sun to Earth.
Bananas are slightly radioactive because they contain potassium-40.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time under specific conditions, known as the triple point.
The human nose can detect over one trillion different scents.
Clouds are actually heavy; a typical cumulus cloud can weigh over a million pounds.
Venus is the only planet in our solar system that rotates clockwise on its axis.
Honey never spoils; archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are still edible.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The largest volcano in the solar system is Olympus Mons on Mars, which is nearly three times the height of Mount Everest.
Sharks have existed on Earth for longer than trees.
The total weight of all ants on Earth is estimated to be roughly equal to the total weight of all humans.
Glass is actually a supercooled liquid, not a solid, though this is a subject of scientific debate.
The fingerprints of koalas are so similar to human fingerprints that they have occasionally been confused at crime scenes.
Oxygen gas is colorless, but in liquid and solid forms, it appears light blue.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
The Earth spins at a speed of about 1,000 miles per hour at the equator.
The moon is drifting away from Earth at a rate of approximately 3.8 centimeters per year.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all the beaches on Earth combined.
Water is the only substance on Earth that naturally exists in all three states: solid, liquid, and gas.
The human body contains enough iron to make a small metal nail.
Gold is so malleable that a single ounce can be beaten into a sheet covering 100 square feet.
The Arctic is melting, but the Antarctic ice sheet contains about 90 percent of the world's ice.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The Eiffel Tower can be 15 centimeters taller during the summer due to thermal expansion of the iron.
A teaspoon of a neutron star would weigh about six billion tons.
The human brain generates enough electricity to power a small lightbulb.
Plants can communicate with each other through underground fungal networks.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum because there are no particles to vibrate.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest living structure and can be seen from space.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
Some types of jellyfish are biologically immortal and can revert to their juvenile state.
The cornea is the only part of the human body that has no blood supply; it gets oxygen directly from the air.
Saturn would float in a bathtub if you could find one big enough, as its density is lower than water.
Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth, but it can be shattered with a hammer.
The deepest part of the ocean is the Mariana Trench, which is deeper than Mount Everest is tall.
There is a planet made mostly of diamond twice the size of Earth.
Humans share about 60 percent of their DNA with bananas.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The surface area of a human lung is roughly the size of a tennis court.
A snail can sleep for three years.
The Sun accounts for 99.86 percent of the total mass of the entire solar system.
The average person walks the equivalent of five times around the world in their lifetime.
Earth's atmosphere is composed of about 78 percent nitrogen and 21 percent oxygen.
A shrimp's heart is located in its head.
Venus's atmosphere is mostly carbon dioxide, creating a runaway greenhouse effect.
The speed of light is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second.
Most of the dust in your home is actually dead skin cells.
The tongue is the strongest muscle in the human body relative to its size.
A day on Mars is called a Sol and is about 40 minutes longer than an Earth day.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes.
A cloud can contain millions of tons of water droplets.
The planet Neptune was discovered using mathematical calculations before it was seen through a telescope.
The oldest known living organism is a bristlecone pine tree over 5,000 years old.
In space, metal can cold-weld together on contact.
The liver is the only human organ capable of regenerating itself completely from as little as 25 percent of its original tissue.
Ants don't have lungs; they breathe through tiny holes in their sides called spiracles.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the known universe.
The color of the sunset on Mars is blue.
A group of owls is called a parliament.
The human body has more bacteria cells than human cells.
Copper is naturally antibacterial.
Sound travels faster through metal than through liquid.
The world's largest desert is Antarctica.
A neutron star is so dense that a teaspoon of its material would weigh billions of tons.
Sharks have a sixth sense called electroreception to detect muscle movements in prey.
Venus rotates so slowly that it takes longer to complete one rotation than to orbit the Sun.
The Earth's magnetic field is generated by the movement of molten iron in its outer core.
A whale's tongue can weigh as much as a whole elephant.
The tallest living tree species is the Coast Redwood.
Sound waves cannot travel through a vacuum.
The human body is made up of about 60 percent water.
There is enough gold in the Earth's core to coat the entire surface of the planet in 1.5 feet of gold.
Some fungi can glow in the dark, a process known as bioluminescence.
The coldest temperature ever recorded on Earth was in Antarctica at -128.6 degrees Fahrenheit.
A lightning bolt is significantly hotter than the surface of the Sun.
The moon has moonquakes caused by gravitational pull from Earth.
A group of crows is called a murder.
The average adult human skeleton consists of 206 bones.
Helium is lighter than air, which is why balloons float.
The most common element in the universe is hydrogen.
The Earth’s atmosphere extends over 600 miles above the surface.
A person can survive for weeks without food, but only a few days without water.
The speed of sound in air is approximately 343 meters per second.
Some sharks must keep swimming to stay alive, a process known as ram ventilation.
Mars has two moons named Phobos and Deimos.
The human eye can distinguish approximately 10 million different colors.
A year on Mercury is only 88 Earth days long.
The deepest point in the ocean is named the Challenger Deep.
Plants convert sunlight into chemical energy through photosynthesis.
The Earth's crust is divided into several large plates that move over the mantle.
A single drop of blood contains millions of red blood cells.
The largest mammal in the world is the blue whale.
Gravity is the force that attracts objects toward each other.
A photon takes up to 100,000 years to travel from the core of the Sun to its surface.
Neutrinos are subatomic particles that can pass through lead a light-year thick without hitting an atom.
The Earth's inner core rotates at a slightly different speed than the rest of the planet.
Liquid nitrogen is cold enough to freeze soft tissue instantly upon contact.
The blue color of the sky is caused by Rayleigh scattering of sunlight by the atmosphere.
Deep-sea hydrothermal vents release mineral-rich water that supports unique life forms independent of sunlight.
Quantum entanglement allows two particles to affect each other instantaneously regardless of distance.
The human retina has about 120 million rod cells for low-light vision and 6 million cone cells for color.
Most of the carbon in the human body was forged in the hearts of dying stars.
The Fibonacci sequence appears frequently in nature, including in the patterns of sunflower seeds and pinecones.
Water molecules are dipolar, meaning they have a positive end and a negative end, allowing them to dissolve many substances.
The atmosphere of Jupiter consists mainly of hydrogen and helium, similar to the composition of the Sun.
Tectonic plates float on the semi-fluid asthenosphere, which allows them to drift over geological time.
The human stomach acid, hydrochloric acid, is strong enough to dissolve razor blades.
Aerogels, also known as frozen smoke, are among the lightest solid materials on Earth.
The speed of light in a vacuum is constant for all observers, regardless of their motion.
DNA helices can be right-handed or left-handed, but most biological DNA is right-handed.
Supernovae produce heavy elements like gold and platinum through rapid neutron capture.
The Earth experiences tides primarily due to the gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun.
Bioluminescence is used by various marine organisms to attract prey, find mates, or deter predators.
A gram of DNA can theoretically store hundreds of terabytes of digital data.
The total number of galaxies in the observable universe is estimated to be around two trillion.
The human body consists of roughly 30 trillion human cells and roughly 39 trillion bacterial cells.
Entropy, a measure of disorder in a system, always increases in an isolated thermodynamic system.
The greenhouse effect is essential for keeping Earth warm enough to support liquid water.
Crystals are solids whose atoms are arranged in a highly ordered, repeating microscopic structure.
The Coriolis effect causes fluids to rotate in different directions in the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
There are more possible neural connections in a human brain than there are stars in the Milky Way.
The half-life of a radioactive isotope is the time required for half of its atoms to decay.
Mars has the longest-lasting dust storms in the solar system, which can cover the entire planet for months.
Graphite and diamond are both composed entirely of carbon but have vastly different structural properties.
The sound frequency range of typical human hearing is 20 hertz to 20,000 hertz.
Pulsars are highly magnetized, rotating neutron stars that emit beams of electromagnetic radiation.
Mitosis is the process of cell division that results in two genetically identical daughter cells.
The speed of sound varies depending on the density and temperature of the medium through which it travels.
A single lightning strike can reach temperatures of 50,000 degrees Fahrenheit.
The Earth's magnetic north pole is constantly shifting and has even flipped entirely in the past.
Osmosis is the spontaneous net movement of solvent molecules through a semi-permeable membrane.
The observable universe is expanding, and distant galaxies are moving away from us at increasing speeds.
A light-year is the distance that light travels in one year, approximately 9.46 trillion kilometers.
Plate tectonics are responsible for the formation of mountain ranges like the Himalayas.
The human heart beats approximately 100,000 times per day.
Sound is a pressure wave that requires a medium, such as air, water, or solids, to propagate.
Photosynthesis produces oxygen as a byproduct, which is essential for aerobic life on Earth.
The density of an object is defined as its mass per unit volume.
Black holes have gravitational pulls so strong that not even light can escape their event horizon.
Enzymes are biological catalysts that speed up chemical reactions within living organisms.
The absolute zero temperature is -273.15 degrees Celsius, where all molecular motion theoretically stops.
There are four fundamental forces in nature: gravity, electromagnetism, the weak nuclear force, and the strong nuclear force.
The Kuiper Belt is a region of the solar system beyond Neptune filled with icy bodies and dwarf planets.
Bacterial endospores can survive extreme heat, radiation, and desiccation for long periods.
The pH scale measures the concentration of hydrogen ions in a solution, with 7 being neutral.
Gamma-ray bursts are the most powerful explosions in the universe, lasting from milliseconds to minutes.
The human skeletal system is responsible for producing blood cells in the bone marrow.
Diffusion is the movement of particles from an area of higher concentration to lower concentration.
Superconductors are materials that can conduct electricity with zero resistance at very low temperatures.
The Oort Cloud is a theoretical cloud of icy objects that surrounds our solar system.
Chromosomes are long DNA molecules that carry part or all of the genetic material of an organism.
A molecule is a group of two or more atoms held together by chemical bonds.
Exoplanets are planets that orbit stars outside of our solar system.
The endocrine system regulates body functions through the secretion of hormones.
Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another of different density.
The chemical symbol for tungsten is W, derived from the German word 'wolfram'.
Sublimation is the transition of a substance directly from a solid to a gas state.
Dark matter does not emit, absorb, or reflect light, but it exerts a gravitational pull on visible matter.
The human immune system uses white blood cells to identify and neutralize pathogens.
A catalyst increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
Nebulae are vast clouds of gas and dust that serve as the birthplaces of stars.
The Doppler effect is the change in frequency of a wave in relation to an observer moving relative to the source.
Neurons communicate with each other through electrochemical signals at junctions called synapses.
Plasma is an ionized gas and is the most common state of matter in the universe.
The term 'atom' comes from the Greek word 'atomos', meaning indivisible.
The troposphere is the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere where all weather occurs.
Proteins are complex macromolecules built from sequences of amino acids.
Reflection occurs when light waves bounce off a surface at an angle equal to the incident angle.
The Sun's corona is significantly hotter than its visible surface, known as the photosphere.
A light-year is a unit of distance, not a unit of time.
The human nervous system is divided into the central and peripheral nervous systems.
Combustion is a high-temperature exothermic chemical reaction between a fuel and an oxidant.
Quasars are extremely bright, distant active galactic nuclei powered by supermassive black holes.
An isotope is a variant of a chemical element with a different number of neutrons.
Tides occur because the moon's gravity pulls harder on the side of the Earth facing it.
The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed.
Mitotic cell division is crucial for growth and tissue repair in multicellular organisms.
Noble gases are found in the last group of the periodic table and are notably inert.
The ozone layer protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation from the Sun.
Supernova explosions create the pressure and heat necessary to fuse elements heavier than iron.
The average temperature of the universe is about 2.7 Kelvin, the leftover heat from the Big Bang.
A chemical reaction involves the breaking and forming of chemical bonds.
The speed of light is the universal speed limit for all energy and matter.
The human circulatory system includes the heart, blood vessels, and blood.
Valence electrons are the electrons in the outermost shell of an atom involved in bonding.
Magnetic fields are produced by electric currents and magnetic moments of elementary particles.
Stars generate energy by fusing hydrogen nuclei into helium in their cores.
The process of natural selection explains how populations adapt to their environment over generations.
Entropy explains why time has an arrow, moving from a state of low disorder to high disorder.
Metabolism includes all the chemical reactions that occur within an organism to maintain life.
Pressure is defined as the force applied perpendicular to the surface of an object per unit area.
The periodic table organizes elements based on their atomic number and chemical properties.
Electrons carry a negative charge, while protons carry a positive charge.
Thermal energy is the kinetic energy of the random motion of atoms and molecules in a substance.
Refraction is used in lenses to focus light, which is fundamental to how cameras and human eyes work.
The process of meiosis produces gametes with half the number of chromosomes of the parent cell.
A light beam consists of photons, which have no mass but carry momentum and energy.
Geothermal energy is heat extracted from the Earth's interior.
Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.
Cellular respiration occurs in the mitochondria to produce ATP, the energy currency of the cell.
Sound waves can undergo reflection, refraction, and diffraction.
A supernova can briefly outshine an entire galaxy.
The greenhouse effect works by trapping infrared radiation emitted from the planet's surface.
Atomic number is the number of protons found in the nucleus of an atom.
The human body regulates its internal environment through a process called homeostasis.
A photon is a quantum of light or other electromagnetic radiation.
The law of universal gravitation describes the attraction between two objects with mass.
DNA structure was discovered by Watson and Crick, with contributions from Rosalind Franklin.
The Earth's crust is the outermost solid layer of the planet.
Conduction is the transfer of heat through direct contact between materials.
Radiation is the transfer of energy through electromagnetic waves, requiring no medium.
Convection is the transfer of heat through the movement of fluids like liquid or gas.
Radioactive decay is a random process at the level of individual atoms.
Acceleration is the rate of change of an object's velocity over time.
Friction is a force that resists the relative motion of surfaces in contact.
Velocity is a vector quantity that specifies both speed and direction.
Work is defined as the force applied to an object multiplied by the displacement in the direction of the force.
Power is the rate at which work is performed or energy is transferred.
The Big Bang theory posits that the universe began as a hot, dense singularity about 13.8 billion years ago.
The atmosphere helps burn up most meteoroids before they reach the surface.
The moon’s tidal forces are slowing down the Earth’s rotation over time.
Light can behave as both a wave and a particle, a concept known as wave-particle duality.
The atomic weight is the average mass of an element's isotopes weighted by their natural abundance.
Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe, making up about 75 percent of normal matter.
A calorie is a unit of energy, specifically the energy needed to raise the temperature of one gram of water by one degree Celsius.
Earth's magnetic field protects the planet's atmosphere from being stripped away by the solar wind.
The human skeleton continues to grow until about the age of 20 to 25.
Bioluminescence in fungi typically functions to attract insects that spread their spores.
Sound waves can be amplified through resonance, the tendency of a system to oscillate at larger amplitudes at specific frequencies.
The speed of sound is faster in warmer air compared to colder air.
A supernova remnant is an expanding shell of gas and dust left behind after a star explodes.
The Milky Way galaxy is estimated to contain between 100 and 400 billion stars.
Cells are the basic structural, functional, and biological units of all known living organisms.
Cell membranes are composed of a lipid bilayer with embedded proteins.
A dominant gene will mask the expression of a recessive gene in an organism's phenotype.
Genotype refers to the genetic makeup of an organism, while phenotype refers to its observable characteristics.
Evolutionary adaptations improve an organism's ability to survive and reproduce in a specific environment.
Natural selection acts on individuals, but evolution occurs at the population level.
The human brain uses about 20 percent of the body's total energy intake.
The cerebellum is responsible for coordination, precision, and accurate timing of movements.
Antibiotics are effective against bacteria but do not work against viruses.
Vaccines train the immune system to recognize and fight specific pathogens without causing the disease.
The greenhouse gas carbon dioxide is naturally produced by respiration and volcanic activity.
Nitrogen makes up the majority of Earth's atmosphere.
The noble gases, like neon and argon, are characterized by their lack of chemical reactivity.
Electronegativity is a measure of an atom's ability to attract a bonding pair of electrons.
Ions are atoms that have lost or gained electrons, resulting in a net electric charge.
Covalent bonds involve the sharing of electron pairs between atoms.
Ionic bonds are formed through the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions.
The melting point of a substance is the temperature at which it changes from solid to liquid.
The boiling point is the temperature at which the vapor pressure of a liquid equals the external pressure.
Solutions are homogeneous mixtures where the solute is uniformly distributed within the solvent.
Suspensions are heterogeneous mixtures where particles are large enough to settle over time.
Chemical kinetics is the study of the rates at which chemical reactions proceed.
Activation energy is the minimum energy required for a chemical reaction to occur.
Equilibrium occurs in a chemical reaction when the rate of the forward reaction equals the rate of the reverse reaction.
Acids increase the concentration of hydrogen ions in water, while bases decrease it.
The atomic nucleus contains protons and neutrons, which together are called nucleons.
Quantum mechanics describes the physical properties of nature at the scale of atoms and subatomic particles.
Relativity explains that time and space are linked, and gravity is the curvature of spacetime.
Superstring theory suggests that fundamental particles are actually tiny vibrating strings.
Dark energy is a mysterious force that is driving the accelerated expansion of the universe.
Cosmic microwave background radiation is the 'afterglow' of the Big Bang.
Black hole evaporation, or Hawking radiation, suggests black holes can lose mass over time.
The Schwarzschild radius is the distance from the center of a non-rotating black hole to its event horizon.
A pulsar's rotation can be so precise that it rivals the accuracy of atomic clocks.
Supermassive black holes reside at the centers of most large galaxies, including our own.
The habitable zone is the range of orbits around a star where liquid water could exist on a planet.
Planetary rings, like those of Saturn, are composed of countless particles of ice and rock.
Venus's surface is essentially a desert, characterized by rocky, volcanic plains.
Mercury's thin atmosphere, or exosphere, is constantly stripped away by the solar wind.
The asteroid belt is a region located between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.
Comets are icy bodies that release gas and dust as they approach the Sun, creating a visible tail.
Meteorites are pieces of debris from space that survive their passage through the atmosphere to land on Earth.
Earth's core consists of a solid inner core and a liquid outer core.
Magma is molten rock found beneath the Earth's surface, while lava is molten rock on the surface.
Sedimentary rocks are formed by the accumulation of minerals and particles over time.
Metamorphic rocks are formed when existing rock is subjected to high heat and pressure.
Igneous rocks are formed from the cooling and solidification of molten rock.
The water cycle is the continuous movement of water on, above, and below the Earth's surface.
Atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude because there is less air pressing down from above.
The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere, the second layer of Earth's atmosphere.
Climate is the long-term pattern of weather in a specific area.
Greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide, methane, water vapor, and nitrous oxide.
Fossil fuels were formed from the remains of ancient organisms buried millions of years ago.
Renewable energy comes from sources that are naturally replenished, such as sunlight and wind.
Biodiversity is the variety of life on Earth, including different species, genetics, and ecosystems.
An ecosystem consists of all the living organisms in a given area interacting with their physical environment.
Trophic levels describe the position an organism occupies in a food chain.
Symbiosis is a long-term interaction between two different biological organisms.
The human genome contains about 3 billion DNA base pairs.
Stem cells have the unique ability to develop into many different cell types in the body.
An antibody is a protein used by the immune system to neutralize foreign invaders.
Neurotransmitters are chemical messengers that transmit signals across a chemical synapse between neurons.
Hormones are chemical substances produced in the body that control and regulate the activity of certain cells or organs.
The endocrine system includes glands like the pituitary, thyroid, and adrenal glands.
Digestion begins in the mouth, where enzymes in saliva start breaking down carbohydrates.
The liver filters toxins from the blood and produces bile to help digest fats.
The kidneys filter blood to remove waste products and maintain fluid balance.
The lungs are responsible for gas exchange, taking in oxygen and releasing carbon dioxide.
Muscles move the body by contracting and pulling on bones, to which they are attached by tendons.
The skin is the largest organ in the human body, acting as a protective barrier.
The human eye uses a lens to focus light onto the retina, which contains light-sensitive cells.
Sound waves enter the ear and cause the eardrum to vibrate, which is then transmitted to the inner ear.
Taste buds on the tongue detect five primary tastes: sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami.
The sense of smell is linked to the limbic system, which also processes emotions and memories.
The human brain is divided into two hemispheres connected by a bundle of nerve fibers called the corpus callosum.
Plasticity is the brain's ability to reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life.
Sleep is essential for cognitive function, memory consolidation, and physical recovery.
A virus is a submicroscopic infectious agent that replicates only inside the living cells of an organism.
Parasitism is a type of symbiotic relationship where one organism benefits at the expense of the other.
Mutualism is a symbiotic relationship where both organisms benefit.
Commensalism is a relationship where one organism benefits while the other is neither helped nor harmed.
Evolution is supported by fossil records, genetic evidence, and anatomical similarities between species.
Extinction is the end of a species, often due to environmental changes or lack of adaptation.
The fossil record provides snapshots of past life and the changes that have occurred over millions of years.
Genetic mutations are changes in the DNA sequence that can lead to variation within a population.
Genetics is the study of genes, genetic variation, and heredity in living organisms.
Inheritance occurs through the passing of genetic information from parents to offspring.
The cell cycle consists of interphase, mitosis, and cytokinesis.
Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis in the cell.
The nucleus acts as the control center of the cell and contains the genetic material.
Chloroplasts are organelles found in plants that perform photosynthesis.
Mitochondria are often referred to as the powerhouse of the cell due to ATP production.
Osmosis is crucial for maintaining the water balance within cells.
Homeostasis ensures that internal conditions remain stable despite changes in the external environment.
The scientific method is a systematic process of observation, experimentation, and analysis.
A hypothesis is a testable explanation for a set of observations.
Scientific theories are well-substantiated explanations based on facts that have been repeatedly tested.
A law of nature describes a consistent and predictable observation in the natural world.
Measurement involves comparing an unknown quantity to a standard unit.
The SI system (International System of Units) provides a standardized set of measurements for science.
Significant figures represent the precision of a measurement.
An error in science can be either systematic, affecting accuracy, or random, affecting precision.
Data analysis uses statistical methods to interpret results and draw conclusions.
Peer review is a process by which scientific work is evaluated by experts before publication.
Technology is the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes.
Engineering uses principles of science and mathematics to design and build structures and systems.
Nanotechnology involves manipulating matter at the atomic or molecular scale.
Robotics is the branch of technology that deals with the design, construction, and operation of robots.
Biotechnology uses biological systems to develop products or processes.
Artificial intelligence simulates human intelligence processes by machines and computer systems.
Information theory studies the quantification, storage, and communication of information.
Cryptography is the practice and study of techniques for secure communication in the presence of adversaries.
A computer uses binary code, consisting of 0s and 1s, to process information.
The internet is a global network of interconnected computers.
Algorithms are sets of rules or instructions followed by a computer to perform tasks.
Data science extracts knowledge and insights from structured and unstructured data.
Mathematics is the language of science, used to describe patterns, relationships, and quantities.
Geometry studies shapes, sizes, and the properties of space.
Calculus is the mathematical study of continuous change.
Statistics is the branch of mathematics that deals with the collection and analysis of data.
Prime numbers are natural numbers greater than 1 that have no positive divisors other than 1 and themselves.
Pi is the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, approximately 3.14159.
Probability measures the likelihood that an event will occur.
The Golden Ratio is a mathematical ratio often found in nature and art.
Set theory is a branch of mathematical logic that studies sets, which are collections of objects.
Algebra is the branch of mathematics that uses symbols to represent numbers in equations.
Trigonometry studies the relationships between the angles and sides of triangles.
Number theory is a branch of pure mathematics devoted to the study of integers.
The fourth dimension is often represented as time in the context of spacetime.
Fractals are complex geometric shapes that exhibit self-similarity at different scales.
Topology studies the properties of space that are preserved under continuous deformations.
Linear algebra is the branch of mathematics concerning linear equations and their representations in vector spaces.
Discrete mathematics deals with countable, distinct structures.
Logic is the study of correct reasoning.
The scientific method relies on the iterative process of testing and refining hypotheses.
Observation is the act of noting and recording phenomena as they occur.
Experiments are conducted to test a specific variable while keeping others constant.
Models are simplified representations used to understand complex phenomena.
The peer review process ensures the quality and credibility of scientific research.
Scientific paradigms are the frameworks of beliefs and practices that guide a scientific field.
Science communication bridges the gap between scientific experts and the general public.
Ethical considerations are central to scientific research involving humans, animals, and the environment.
Open science promotes the sharing of research, data, and findings to accelerate discovery.
Citizens of science initiatives allow the public to contribute to scientific data collection and research.
Scientific inquiry is driven by curiosity and the desire to understand the world.
Critical thinking is an essential skill in evaluating evidence and reaching conclusions.
Skepticism is the habit of questioning claims until sufficient evidence is provided.
Bias is a potential source of error in both the collection and interpretation of scientific data.
Objectivity is the ideal of approaching scientific problems without personal prejudice.
Replication is the process of repeating experiments to confirm the validity of the results.
Scientific progress often involves building upon the work of previous generations.
Technology can sometimes create new problems, necessitating responsible and thoughtful innovation.
Sustainability aims to meet the needs of the present without compromising future generations.
Environmental science studies the impact of human activities on the natural world.
Conservation biology aims to protect and restore biodiversity and ecosystems.
Climate change is one of the most pressing challenges facing the global scientific community.
Energy efficiency is a key component of sustainable technology development.
Materials science is focused on the discovery and design of new materials for various applications.
Space exploration provides insights into the origin and evolution of the universe.
Medicine uses scientific knowledge to diagnose, treat, and prevent disease.
The future of science will likely be shaped by the convergence of diverse disciplines like AI, biotechnology, and physics.
The density of the Earth increases significantly toward the center, reaching a maximum in the inner core.
Liquid crystals, used in displays, share properties of both liquids and solid crystals.
A standard deck of 52 cards has more possible arrangements than atoms on Earth.
Photosynthesis is essentially the conversion of radiant energy into stable chemical energy.
Tectonic plate movement is primarily driven by mantle convection and slab pull.

Venus is covered by thick clouds of sulfuric acid, reflecting most incoming sunlight.
The human genome is about 99.9 percent identical across all people.
Electromagnetism is one of the four fundamental forces that govern the behavior of subatomic particles.
The freezing point of water is lowered by the addition of salt, a process called freezing-point depression.
Supernovae are the primary source of heavy elements scattered throughout the cosmos.
A light-minute is approximately 18 million kilometers.
The Earth's crust is thinnest under the oceans and thickest under continental mountain ranges.
Human teeth are the only part of the body that cannot heal themselves.

The atmosphere of Mars is 95 percent carbon dioxide, but it is far too thin to retain much heat.

The average depth of the ocean is about 3.7 kilometers.
Binary stars, where two stars orbit a common center of mass, are very common in the galaxy.

The chemical element mercury is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature.
A light-year is roughly 63,241 astronomical units.
Tsunamis are usually caused by underwater earthquakes or volcanic eruptions displacing massive amounts of water.
Human skin replaces its entire outer layer roughly every four weeks.
Relativity implies that mass and energy are interchangeable via the famous equation $E = mc^2$.

The Earth's magnetic field reverses polarity every few hundred thousand years on average.
There are three main types of muscles in the human body: skeletal, cardiac, and smooth.

Saturn's rings are primarily made of billions of chunks of ice and rock ranging from dust to boulders.
The atomic weight of an element is approximately the number of protons plus neutrons in the nucleus.
Erosion is the process by which surface materials are worn away and transported by natural forces.

Helium was first detected in the Sun's spectrum before it was found on Earth.
The speed of light is the same for all observers, regardless of the motion of the source or the observer.
Earth's orbit around the Sun is slightly elliptical, not a perfect circle.
The human body contains about 5 liters of blood on average.
Mass and weight are different; mass is the amount of matter, while weight is the force of gravity on that mass.

Chemical compounds have properties different from the elements that compose them.
Frequency is the number of occurrences of a repeating event per unit time, measured in Hertz.

The chemical element gold is extremely unreactive and does not tarnish easily.
Refraction occurs because light travels at different speeds in different materials.
The Earth's mantle is solid, but flows slowly over geological time due to extreme pressure and temperature.
The human heart is a dual-pump system that sends blood to the lungs and the rest of the body.
Force is a vector quantity defined as mass times acceleration, $F = ma$.
Exoplanets are often discovered using the transit method, observing the slight dimming of a star's light.
A solution is saturated when it cannot dissolve any more solute at a given temperature.
Wavelength is the distance between consecutive peaks of a wave.

Genetic engineering allows scientists to modify the DNA of organisms to produce desired traits.
The chemical symbol for lead is Pb, from the Latin word 'plumbum'.

The lithosphere includes the Earth's crust and the topmost part of the mantle.
White blood cells are a key part of the immune system, defending against infections and disease.
Energy can be kinetic, potential, thermal, chemical, nuclear, or electromagnetic.
The Moon is in synchronous rotation with Earth, which is why we only ever see one side of it.

Amplitude is the maximum displacement or distance moved by a point on a vibrating body or wave.
The Big Bang is not an explosion in space, but rather the rapid expansion of space itself.
Ribonucleic acid, or RNA, is a molecule essential for coding, decoding, regulation, and expression of genes.
Sodium metal reacts violently with water to produce hydrogen gas and sodium hydroxide.
Lenses function by changing the direction of light rays through refraction.
The Earth's inner core is solid, despite its high temperature, due to immense pressure.
Red blood cells carry oxygen throughout the body using a protein called hemoglobin.

The electromagnetic spectrum includes radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, X-rays, and gamma rays.

Mutations can be beneficial, harmful, or neutral depending on their effect on the organism's fitness.

The biosphere includes all regions of the Earth occupied by living organisms.
Antibodies are specific to the antigens they bind to, much like a lock and key.

The Sun's energy output is so vast that it releases more energy in a second than humanity has consumed in its history.
Bases feel slippery and turn red litmus paper blue.
Radio waves have the longest wavelengths in the electromagnetic spectrum.
The universe is approximately 13.8 billion years old.
Transcription is the process of copying a segment of DNA into RNA.

The lifecycle of a star is determined primarily by its initial mass.
A neutralization reaction occurs when an acid and a base react to form water and a salt.
X-rays have shorter wavelengths than ultraviolet light and can penetrate soft tissues.

Polarization is the property of light waves where their vibrations occur in a single plane.
The Earth's crust is mostly composed of silicate minerals.

Potential energy is stored energy based on an object's position or state.
